mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes CLS 450 / 500 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned CLS 450/500 (M256) engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Fitting adds £2,000–£3,500 depending on location and workshop rates. Main dealer new engines start from £9,000–£15,000+ supply only. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes CLS 450 / 500 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M256 is considered 'robust and reliable' when serviced regularly. It marked Mercedes' return to the inline-six layout, combining smoothness with modern efficiency. However, it has documented issues: coolant pump and 48V system failures (subject to recall), timing chain noise on cold start, wiring harness faults, and carbon buildup. With proper maintenance, these engines can be reliable, but the coolant pump issue is a genuine concern.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes CLS 450 / 500?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Coolant pump / 48V system failure — electric line chafing on the engine fan; subject to recall, (2) Timing chain noise on cold start — rattling for a few seconds on startup, (3) Wiring harness and electrical faults — causing misfires and ECU issues, and (4) Carbon buildup on intake valves — causing rough idle and misfires.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes CLS 450 / 500 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £18,000–£30,000 CLS, a £5,000–£7,500 recon is 20–40% of the car's value. For a clean example with full service history, replacement is often worth it given the CLS's strong retained values and the M256's reputation when properly maintained. However, if the car has other issues (air suspension, electrical faults, accident damage), evaluate total repair costs before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes CLS 450 / 500 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The CLS 450 and 500 use the M256 engine code — a 3.0-litre turbocharged inline-six with 48V mild hybrid. The CLS 450 produces 362 hp (270 kW) and 369 lb-ft (500 Nm); the CLS 500 produces 381–388 hp and 384 lb-ft in select markets. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Is the CLS 450 / 500 coolant pump issue covered by a recall?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — a recall (NHTSA campaign number: 22V733000) affects certain CLS vehicles with the M256 engine. The electric line for the power supply of the engine coolant pump might contact the engine fan and chafe on it. Check with your dealer whether your vehicle is affected and if the recall work has been completed. This is a free fix at the dealership.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a CLS 450/500 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The 48V mild-hybrid system and complex wiring add labour time.

problems.title: Coolant Pump & 48V System Failure
problems.symptoms: Erratic engine cooling, warning lights, coolant loss, overheating, electric line chafing on the engine fan
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — a recall affects certain CLS vehicles with the M256 engine
problems.replacementCost: £5,500–£10,500 (if ignored — overheating can cause engine damage)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the coolant warning appears or the engine overheats, stop driving immediately

problems.recommendation: The electric line for the power supply of the engine coolant pump might contact the engine fan and chafe on it, leading to electrical faults. Coolant pump and 48V hybrid system issues are a known weakness on the M256. A recall (NHTSA campaign number: 22V733000) affects certain vehicles. If you experience erratic cooling or warning lights, have the coolant pump and wiring inspected immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant pump failure guide → /failures/m256-coolant-pump-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Timing Chain Noise on Cold Start
problems.symptoms: Rattling or ticking noise from the engine for a few seconds on cold start, metallic rattle that subsides as oil pressure builds, "marbles in a can" sound
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles — can occur earlier on some examples
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,000 (timing chain tensioner inspection and replacement) / £2,000–£4,000 (full timing chain kit)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: Timing chain noise on cold start is a reported issue on the M256 engine. A worn or stretched chain and weak tensioner cause a metallic rattling sound for a few seconds after startup. Failed guides can cause the chains to jump, skip a tooth, and potentially snap. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, have the chain tensioners and guides inspected immediately. A £800–£2,000 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£10,500 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide → /failures/m256-timing-chain/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Wiring Harness & Electrical System Faults
problems.symptoms: Check engine light, misfires on specific cylinders, rough running, reduced power, engine speed sensor faults
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — some owners report issues within the first few years
problems.repairCost: £500–£2,000 (wiring harness repair or replacement; ECU diagnosis)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if warning lights appear or misfires develop

problems.recommendation: Multiple owners have reported wiring harness issues on the M256 engine. One CLS 450 owner reported a short circuit due to the copper wiring covers becoming dry and falling out. Another owner reported ignition wiring deformation causing misfires. A known issue with the m256 engine harness can cause ECU failure for ignition coils. If you experience misfires or electrical faults, have the wiring harness and ECU inspected by a specialist with genuine MB STAR diagnostic equipment.

failureLink.label: Read the full wiring harness failure guide → /failures/m256-wiring-harness/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfires, hesitation during acceleration, reduced power, poor fuel economy
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: Like all direct-injection engines, the M256 is prone to carbon buildup on intake valves. Mercedes recommends walnut blasting every ~60,000 miles. If you experience rough idle, misfires or hesitation, have the intake valves inspected. Regular use of premium fuel and occasional spirited driving can help mitigate carbon buildup.

failureLink.label: Read the full carbon buildup guide → /failures/carbon-buildup-intake/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

buyingChecks.title: Buying This CLS 450 / 500? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes using MB-approved oil
* No cold-start rattling — this is a critical warning sign
* Recall work completed (coolant pump wiring harness)
* Remainder of manufacturer warranty or extended warranty in place
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle with no documented timing chain work
* Check engine light or misfire codes with no diagnosis
* Coolant warning light or overheating history
* Poor service history — the M256 is a complex engine that needs proper maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The CLS 450 and 500 are powered by the M256 — Mercedes' return to the inline-six layout, delivering 362–388 hp with 48V mild-hybrid assistance. The engine is considered "robust and reliable" when serviced regularly. However, it has documented weaknesses: coolant pump and 48V system failures (subject to recall), timing chain noise on cold start, wiring harness faults, and carbon buildup. Buy a well-maintained example with full service history and no rattling. Without history? Proceed with caution — the repair bills can be substantial.

cta.label: → Compare CLS 450 / 500 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes CLS 450 or 500 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking from the engine (timing chain warning — this is critical)
* Coolant System — Check for recall completion (coolant pump wiring harness); check for coolant loss
* Service History — Including the M256 engine and confirmed oil change intervals (MB 229.51 spec)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for timing, misfire or electrical codes
* Wiring Harness — Any misfires or electrical faults? Check for ignition wiring deformation
* Carbon Buildup — Any hesitation or rough idle? (Intake valve carbon)
* 48V System — Check for any hybrid system warnings
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
